in: Data-driven Scenario Model for the unclear-requirements scenario
Active scenario slice navigation with stepwise command choices
Lightweight React state and SVG interaction in the independent Vite app
Detail-panel alternatives, continuation routes, source badges, and local validation checklist
out: Backend, LLM service, login, real status.json parser, graph library
Expansion of downstream quality or knowledge pipelines beyond route choices

summary: Deliver Phase 1 as a three-wave MVP: first establish typed scenario data and validation helpers outside JSX, then wire active step navigation and SVG path highlighting, then complete the detail panel with continuations, citation badges, and a local checklist.
approach: Keep the implementation inside the existing Vite React app structure, primarily `src/main.tsx` and `src/styles.css`.
Model scenario nodes, edges, steps, choices, citations, continuation routes, and checklist items as typed constants outside React rendering components.
Use React state for selected scenario, active step, selected node, and checklist completion; avoid graph libraries and runtime command execution.
Make every workflow concept in the UI expose purpose, input, output, and next action, with alternatives and citation status confined to the detail panel.
Validate with local helper checks for broken step references and citation coverage plus the baseline `npm run build`.

pressure_pass.result: Scenario fixture must include source refs with status values and validation helpers must expose missing citation coverage before UI work proceeds. This reduces the risk that TASK-003 adds decorative badges without data backing.

constraints: Use TypeScript, React function components, and Vite.
Keep first graph implementation lightweight with React state and SVG.
Scenario data must live outside React rendering components.
Alternatives, terminal routes, source badges, and validation checklist belong in the detail panel.
Citation policy: maestro-flow command definitions first, guide docs second, Codex skill mirrors for platform checks.
Do not reuse maestro-flow/docs-site or modify the maestro-flow submodule.
Do not introduce backend, LLM service, login, real status.json parser, or graph library.
Run `npm run build` after frontend structural changes.
